item.title: 3D-Printed Titanium Chainmail Fabric
item.description: It was created using Direct Metal Laser Sintering (DMLS), a technique that fuses titanium powder with a laser to form strong, corrosion-resistant structures, often used in biomedical and aerospace applications.
item.dc:creator: bohund

item.title: OG Meme Dump 250425
item.dc:creator: lem888

item.title: It's been two years of doing Funny Friday! Here are some of my favorites from year 2 (Video heavy)
item.description:
Hi everyone, I recently noticed that it's been about 2 years that I've been doing Funny Friday, in which I put together humor dumps of different things and post them on Fridays to try to end the work week on a positive note. So to celebrate, this dump is going to be some of my personal favorites that I've posted since last April. I hope you enjoy them as much as I did. As always there will be a link in the comments to a private folder with the stuff that isn't necessarily SFW, so if you want to see that make sure you look for the link in the comments.
She went straight from Baldur's Gate 3 devil to the midwestern dirty talk.
Shoutout to @mydadisdracula
Hope you had a good Friday and some laughs. Have a great weekend
item.dc:creator: PoliticalWanderer

item.title: I took Physics twice in high school (and passed both times)
item.description: True story. There was an end of year trip to Six Flags, and I got to go twice. I think this is a representation of Newton's 3rd Law Of Motion, but I could be wrong. It's been a while. Six Flags Magic Mountain (CA, USA) info: https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Six_Flags_Magic_Mountain

item.title: First world problems of a 36 year old woman
item.description:
I never went to college. I barely finished high-school. I was never a great student. I started working right away to support my family and just never had the money or opportunity until now. I lost my job at the beginning of the month (company bankrupted) and I'm honestly tired of working odd entry level jobs and working my way up to better positions only have to start over at a new job for whatever reason. I'm also tired of hiring managers telling me "we've decided to go with someone more qualified" before they've even interviewed me. Apparently, experience doesn't mean much anymore if you don't have a fancy college education to back it up. I'm considering going into the healthcare field. Either as an admin, health document professional, or medical transcriptionist. The course I plan to take covers all of this. My bf says he will support me financially so I don't have to worry about working. It feels weird. He makes more money than I ever have and I know he can easily do so, but it feels really weird for me to be supported when I've always been the one to support myself, my family, or at the very minimum, pay my half of life expenses (rent, groceries, etc) I just don't know. I feel too old for this, but I also feel like I NEED to do this unless I want to work retail, fast food or warehouse jobs the rest of my life. Also terrified I'm going to fail, because... as I said, I've never been a great student. I'm not dumb, I just never functioned well in a classroom setting. I also had a horrible relationship with math. Still do. I'm just so uncertain. Does anyone have any success stories of going to college late in life?
Thanks for listening to me moan about my first world/old people problems. Here's Rumi baking in the window for cat tax.
item.dc:creator: Semotion

item.title: What Happened Today #85
item.description: ?⚖️ A two-year-old American citizen was sent to Honduras after ICE ignored her family’s legal plan to keep her safely in the U.S. and exiled her alongside her undocumented mother. A conservative federal judge is furious. Meanwhile, a Wisconsin judge was arrested, cuffed, and detained for telling ICE to get the right warrant for a man in her courtroom and refusing to improperly let them take him into custody. This isn’t politics. It’s a war on the rule of law. Courts, inspectors general, and a May Day wall of workers are pushing back. Add your voice to the fight before there’s no one left to stand because first they came for the other people. Don’t trust the algorithm. Get this in your inbox every night, free and private, so you never miss what’s really going on. Add your email at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-collective-bargaining 2. https://go.govbrief.today/pride-kennedy-center 3. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-arrested 4. https://go.govbrief.today/subpoena-reporters 5. https://go.govbrief.today/rochester-sued-sanctuary 6. https://go.govbrief.today/UCBerkeley-investigation 7. https://go.govbrief.today/wikipedia-ed-martin 8. https://go.govbrief.today/student-visa 9. https://go.govbrief.today/salmonella-poultry 10. https://go.govbrief.today/doge-americorps 11. https://go.govbrief.today/bogden-taylor 12. https://go.govbrief.today/walczak-pardon 13. https://go.govbrief.today/hatch-reinterpret 14. https://go.govbrief.today/indigenous-mascots 15. https://go.govbrief.today/usaid-tech-devices 16. https://theintercept.com/2025/04/25/hegseth-defense-policy-board-pentagon-tucker-carlson/ 17. https://go.govbrief.today/womens-health-initiative 18. https://go.govbrief.today/irs-records-ig 19. https://go.govbrief.today/may-day-strong

item.title: I had the BEST night tonight
item.description:
I went to my nephew’s 12th birthday party today after work and my brother dropped off a ton of boiled crawfish and potatoes and Brussels sprouts and garlic and it was only the first third! I usually make it a point to go home as soon as work is over but this was worth the detour. Tons of kids and lots of drinks for the parents and more food than anyone could eat. My whole family was there - cousins and grandparents and in-laws…it was really, really nice
I was super happy to come home to the kitten room, of course. This is my happy place. Mr. Raindrop is on the mattress?? Purrsephone is nursing the other babies right in front of me? I could not be happier. The last few weeks of working for the federal government have been…hard. But today was great. I am so happy right now.
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
